name: When is it worth it to hire a lawyer for a forklift injury vs just letting the insurance company handle it?

mainEntity.name: When is it worth it to hire a lawyer for a forklift injury vs just letting the insurance company handle it?

acceptedAnswer.text: Hire a lawyer if you have a serious injury, a denied claim, or if the insurer is trying to stop your medical treatment. If you have permanent damage or need surgery, a lawyer ensures you aren\'t lowballed.
acceptedAnswer.description:
You should hire a lawyer if your injuries are serious (requiring surgery or long-term care), if your claim is denied, or if your employer is pressuring you to return to work before you are ready. For a minor scrape that heals in two days, you probably don\'t need one. But forklift accidents often involve "crush syndrome" or spinal issues that insurance companies love to downplay.

A lawyer is especially important if there is a possibility that a third party (like the forklift manufacturer) was at fault, as those claims are filed in civil court, not the labor commission.

Hire a lawyer if:
- The insurance company denies that the accident happened at work.
- You are being told you don\'t need surgery that your doctor recommended.
- You have permanent symptoms that will keep you from warehouse work.

Most work comp lawyers work on "contingency," meaning they only get paid a percentage of the final settlement. You don\'t pay anything upfront.

Our firm provides a free consultation to tell you honestly if you have a case worth pursuing or if you can handle it yourself.
